prompt: Which statement best captures what AI governance is for in an organisation?
choices: It guarantees the AI model can never make a mistake
It makes the risks of AI use visible and assigns an owner and control to each one
It is purely a technical task handled entirely by engineers
It replaces the need for any human review of AI output

prompt: Select ALL of the following that are legitimate AI governance controls.
choices: An acceptable-use policy stating what data and tasks AI may be used for
An AI use register inventorying where AI is used, with what data and risk tier
Audit logging of prompts, outputs, and decisions for accountability
Choosing whichever AI vendor has the lowest price regardless of its data terms

prompt: A team plans to send customer names and order history to an external AI provider. Which TWO risks are most directly raised, and which controls answer them? Select ALL that apply.
choices: Data privacy / PII risk, answered by redaction, an acceptable-use policy, and a no-training contract clause
IP & licensing / output-ownership risk, answered by reviewing the vendor contract
The risk that the model's context window is too small, answered by a larger model
Hardware failure risk, answered by buying faster servers

prompt: A Business Analyst is asked to support AI governance for a new hiring-screening tool. Which task is the BEST fit for that role?
choices: Writing the PII-redaction code that strips identifiers before prompts are sent
Mapping each AI use to the data it touches and the regulations that apply, feeding the AI use register and risk tiering
Approving the final go-live decision for the whole product
Designing the model's neural network architecture
